management_notes:
Nolan Watson co-founded Sandstorm Gold in 2008, previously serving as CFO of Silver Wheaton (now Wheaton Precious Metals). At the time of the Royal Gold acquisition announcement in July 2025, Watson personally held approximately 1,359,065 shares worth ~USD $22M. Co-founder David Awram also maintained meaningful equity. This co-founder-led structure provided stronger alignment than a typical hired management team at a royalty company of SSL's scale.

Management demonstrated high-quality execution in assembling a 230+ royalty and stream portfolio with 41 producing mines, growing annual attributable GEO from zero at founding in 2008 to a guided 65,000–80,000 GEO in 2025. The company achieved record Q2 2025 revenue of USD $51.4M and a record operating margin of $2,509/GEO sold — just months before the deal announcement — demonstrating operational momentum was intact at time of sale. Management executed on debt reduction and dividend sustainability following the dilutive 2022 Horizon Copper equity issuance.

Negative alignment signal: Watson had reportedly been reducing his personal holding in the period before the deal announcement, which is a weak-form governance concern even if legally permissible. This is scored alongside the positive co-founder history. NOTE: This is a historical scorecard — the SSL management team no longer manages the company as a standalone entity following the October 20, 2025 acquisition close.
geology_notes:
IMPORTANT FRAMEWORK NOTE: Sandstorm Gold was a royalty and streaming company, not a direct mining operator. The Verdict Framework's Geology factor is designed to assess NI 43-101 compliant mineral resource and reserve estimates at directly operated projects. That scoring rubric does not apply to a royalty vehicle. This factor has been adapted to assess portfolio quality and production profile instead. The score of 3/5 carries lower confidence than a direct-operator assessment.

The SSL royalty portfolio at time of acquisition comprised approximately 230 royalties and streams globally, with 41 underlying mines in active production. The 2025 revenue mix was approximately 75% gold and 87% precious metals overall — a gold-dominant profile. Key growth royalties included: MARA copper-gold (Argentina), Hod Maden gold-copper (Turkey), Great Bear (Ontario, acquired by Kinross for Red Lake area development), Platreef PGM (South Africa, Ivanhoe Mines), and Warintza copper-gold (Ecuador, Solaris Resources). These underlie high-quality, multi-million-ounce development projects.

Production trajectory was compelling: 65,000–80,000 GEO guided for full-year 2025, with a stated target of approximately 150,000 GEO by 2030. Royalty holders carry no direct geological, capex, or operational risk — production economics are defined at contract inception. The diversified, 41-mine producing base mitigates single-asset concentration risk that plagues junior explorers. No NI 43-101 Technical Reports are applicable to SSL directly.
capital_notes:
For Q2 2025 (the last full quarter before the acquisition announcement), Sandstorm reported record revenue of USD $51.4M and realized an average margin of $2,509 per GEO sold — implying operating margins of approximately 65–70%. On a trailing annualised basis, this tracks to approximately $200M+ in revenue and $130M+ in operating cash flow. The royalty and streaming model is capital-light by design: after the initial stream or royalty purchase payment, ongoing capital requirements are near-zero and there is no direct exposure to mining cost inflation.

Sandstorm had been focused on debt reduction and dividend sustainability in the years preceding the acquisition, following the capital-intensive 2022 Horizon Copper transaction. Specific year-end 2025 balance sheet figures (cash, drawn debt) were not obtained in this review, limiting precision on this factor. The acquisition price of approximately $3.5B by Royal Gold implicitly validates sound financial fundamentals — a distressed balance sheet would have attracted a lower premium or a debt-restructuring condition. The royalty business model structurally warrants a cleaner capital structure score than an operator.

Historical caveat: all capital structure analysis reflects a closed, delisted company. Scores are for historical record purposes only.
catalyst_notes:
As an acquired and delisted entity, SSL has no investable catalysts from the current date forward. This factor is scored based on the pre-acquisition situation as of July 2025. The Royal Gold acquisition itself was the terminal catalyst, completing at 17–21% premium to the pre-announcement price on October 20, 2025. Shareholders received 0.0625 of a Royal Gold share per SSL share — at approximately $165 USD per Royal Gold share at close, this implied approximately $10.31 USD per SSL share.

Pre-announcement organic catalysts included: (1) MARA copper-gold project in Argentina advancing toward feasibility and construction decision; (2) Hod Maden gold-copper project in Turkey (Lidya Madencilik operator) moving toward production; (3) Great Bear royalty benefiting from Kinross's Red Lake area consolidation; (4) gradual portfolio ramp toward 150,000 GEO by 2030. These represented genuine growth levers but none constituted a near-term binary catalyst.

For historical scoring: SSL's pre-deal catalyst pipeline was moderate — credible long-range growth but no imminent single-asset inflection that would have driven a near-term re-rating independent of the acquisition premium. The modest 17–21% deal premium confirms the market had already priced in much of the growth narrative.
acquisition_notes:
The Royal Gold all-share deal valued each SSL share at approximately USD $10.31 (0.0625 × approximately $165 Royal Gold share price at close), representing a 17% premium to the closing price on July 3, 2025 and a 21% premium to the 20-day VWAP. The total deal value was approximately $3.5B USD. At the pre-announcement price of approximately $8.80 USD per SSL share, the company was trading at roughly 0.85–0.95x analyst-estimated NAV — a modest discount, not a deep discount.

For royalty company acquisition comparisons: the $3.5B transaction valued SSL at approximately 12–14x trailing revenue and 60–70x trailing EBITDA — broadly in line with royalty company M&A precedents (Franco-Nevada, Royal Gold, and Wheaton trade at 20–30x EBITDA at the peak). The 17–21% premium and absence of a competing bid suggest the deal represented fair-value pricing for SSL shareholders, not a transformative windfall. Long-term holders who carried the stock through the dilutive 2022 Horizon Copper equity issuance received only a partial recovery of lost ground.

Note on framework application: the Acquisition Value factor is designed to assess P/NAV discount for junior miners vs. comparable acquisition multiples. The royalty company model carries structurally different valuation conventions. nav_per_share is set to USD $10.31 (implied deal consideration), and current_price is null as the company no longer trades. The 3/5 score reflects fair-value acquisition with modest pre-deal discount — not a deep-discount setup.
analyst_summary:
IMPORTANT: Sandstorm Gold Ltd. (TSX: SSL; NYSE: SAND) was acquired by Royal Gold, Inc. and delisted from both the TSX and NYSE on or around October 22, 2025. This scorecard is a historical record only — SSL is not investable as a standalone security. The composite score of 15/25 maps to WATCH by formula, but the verdict is manually overridden to AVOID since the company cannot be purchased.

At time of acquisition, Sandstorm represented a well-managed, co-founder-led royalty and streaming company with a growing portfolio of 230+ royalties across 41 producing mines, strong Q2 2025 operating momentum (record $51.4M revenue, $2,509/GEO margin), and a credible growth trajectory toward 150,000 GEO by 2030. The framework scoring has limited precision because the Verdict Framework is designed for junior mining operators, not royalty vehicles — geology and acquisition scoring required significant adaptation, and confidence is low for those two factors specifically. The overall confidence is marked low accordingly.

Key lesson from this transaction: Sandstorm's 17-year patient royalty accumulation created sufficient portfolio scale and quality to attract a strategic acquirer at a modest premium. The 17–21% deal premium confirms SSL was trading near fair value at time of announcement — not at a deep discount. Investors who held SSL through the 2022 Horizon Copper dilution received fair-value compensation, not a premium exit. For investors now tracking Sandstorm's business going forward, the successor holding is Royal Gold, Inc. (NASDAQ: RGLD; TSX: RGL).
